message:
refactor(fnp-ufunc): collapse remainder/fmod onto elementwise_binary

Two small consolidation passes plus a follow-up bug bead.

remainder() and fmod() in crates/fnp-ufunc/src/lib.rs each had a
~25-line bespoke implementation that built the broadcast pair via
UFuncArray::broadcast_arrays, walked the value vectors with .zip(),
and special-cased the divide-by-zero (-> NaN) and sign-correction
(Python vs C semantics) branches inline. The same logic was already
expressed once in BinaryOp::Remainder / BinaryOp::Fmod inside the
elementwise_binary dispatch table — the bespoke versions were left
behind during a partial migration.

Replace each function body with a single delegating call:

  pub fn remainder(x1, x2) -> Result<UFuncArray, UFuncError> {
      x1.elementwise_binary(x2, BinaryOp::Remainder)
  }
  pub fn fmod(x1, x2) -> Result<UFuncArray, UFuncError> {
      x1.elementwise_binary(x2, BinaryOp::Fmod)
  }

Net diff: -45 lines, zero behavior change. The dispatch path is
the canonical one used by every other binary ufunc in this file,
and centralizing means a future fix to the divide-by-zero branch
or the sign-correction logic only needs to land once.

bead: file franken_numpy-qhvl (open, P1) tracking a separate bug
surfaced while exercising fnp.mod for the bitwise-port follow-up:
fnp.mod returns float64 even when both inputs are int64, but
NumPy preserves the integer dtype. Recorded for the next agent;
the fix likely lives in the BinaryOp::Remainder dispatch arm
(needs to consult integer_sidecar and emit an integer-typed
result when both operands carry one).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Include oracle test covering: float scalar, negative scalar, 1-D mixed-sign array, integer input, complex input, 2-D array, and dtype preservation across float/int/complex.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:33:26.442663085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:47:21.808348346Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:47:21.806427907Z","close_reason":"Closed by cc-numpy — wrapper + oracle parity test delivered in commit 764f448 (verified via git log --grep br-franken_numpy-qd84).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-qhjn","title":"[MEDIUM] fnp-linalg svd/qr/norm bit-exact parity blocker for hswf ports","description":"hswf asks to port np.linalg.svd/qr/norm/etc. from numpy passthrough to fnp_linalg native kernels. Probe (85645a2): fnp_linalg::svd_mxn produces singular values that differ from LAPACK by ~2 ULPs (e.g. 9.508032000695726 vs numpy 9.508032000695724).

The existing parity oracle tests
  svd_matches_numpy_namedtuple_array_and_error_paths
  qr_matches_numpy_*
  norm_matches_numpy_*
assert via strict tolist-repr equality, which cannot tolerate the ULP drift.

Fix direction (one of):
  1. Port an SVD/QR algorithm that bit-matches LAPACK (Householder reflections with identical pivoting). Hard.
  2. Relax the parity oracles to numpy.allclose-level tolerance. Fast but reduces test strictness.
  3. Ship fnp-linalg kernels only for narrow fast paths that demonstrably bit-match (e.g. det_nxn, inv_nxn, eigvalsh already do). Keep passthrough for everything else.

Current choice: (3). Leaves svd / qr / norm / cond / tensorsolve / lstsq / tensorinv / solve_triangular as passthroughs until a kernel with bit-exact LAPACK parity lands.

Blocks: franken_numpy-hswf ambition to have 'fnp-linalg own these behaviors'.

Not a correctness bug; current behavior (passthrough) is already correct — this is tracking the implementation gap that blocks completing hswf.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T16:54:22.220690793Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:28:55.988304015Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:28:55.985284790Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-qhmv","title":"[HIGH] fnp-python ma_count test: AttributeError on direct .dtype on scalar int result","description":"fnp-python test tests::ma_count_matches_numpy_across_axis_and_keepdims FAILS with AttributeError: 'int' object has no attribute 'dtype'.

## Reproducer

export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_numpy_cc
cargo test -p fnp-python --lib ma_count_matches_numpy_across_axis_and_keepdims

## Root cause

np.ma.count with axis=None returns a plain Python int (not a 0-D numpy array). The test body at lib.rs (multiple call sites) uses direct .getattr("dtype")?.str()?.to_string() pattern instead of the scalar-tolerant assert_array_matches_numpy helper that ma_count's OTHER test paths use.

Context: commit a61593e (cc-numpy, 2026-04-22) made the central assert_array_matches_numpy helper scalar-tolerant by checking .getattr("dtype").is_err() before recursing. That fix resolves the helper-based assertions, but does NOT help inline .getattr("dtype")? calls that the test has for extra dtype-name assertions.

## Suggested fix

Either:
(a) Guard each inline .dtype call with a scalar check:
    if ours_int.getattr("dtype").is_ok() {
        let ours_dtype = ours_int.getattr("dtype")?.str()?.to_string();
        ...
    }

(b) Extract inline dtype assertions into a helper that mirrors assert_array_matches_numpy's scalar tolerance.

(c) Route ma_count's axis=None case through a different assertion path that doesn't check dtype (since Python ints don't have one).

## Severity rationale

[HIGH] — this is a pure test-side bug (the wrapper itself returns the correct Python int matching numpy). Blocks the test battery from being green. Masks possible regressions in wrapper behavior because the test fails early on the axis=None case.

## Ownership

Test body added by cod-numpy during hswc rust-port work. Fix is mechanical — apply the scalar-tolerance pattern to inline dtype asserts (4-5 sites in this test).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:43:01.433456511Z","created_by":"cc-numpy","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:50:15.486414105Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:50:15.484543957Z","close_reason":"Closed by cc-numpy — fixed in commit 45d81cb. Root cause: assert_array_matches_numpy helper only handled symmetric scalar-both-sides case; asymmetric ours=numpy-scalar theirs=python-int threw AttributeError on shape lookup. Fix: added .item() normalisation so both sides compare via repr after reducing to Python scalar form. Verified: ma_count_matches_numpy_across_axis_and_keepdims 1 ok / 0 failed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review","scalar-tolerant","test-bug"]}

 {"id":"franken_numpy-qkbf","title":"Restore fail-fast invariant handling in unique_counts/unique_inverse/unique_all","description":"Current worktree change in crates/fnp-ufunc/src/lib.rs replaces impossible-state unwrap_or_else(unreachable!) guards in unique_counts/unique_inverse/unique_all with silent empty-array fallbacks. These wrappers request the corresponding arrays from unique_with_info, so returning empty arrays would mask internal invariant breaks and violate NumPy-style semantics.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-29T01:56:24.561784002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-29T01:58:08.648898780Z","closed_at":"2026-03-29T01:58:08.648641733Z","close_reason":"Restored fail-fast invariant handling in unique_counts/unique_inverse/unique_all; cargo fmt --check and rch exec cargo test -p fnp-ufunc unique_with_info -- --nocapture passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
